purpose: Historical applied receipt for the 6 reversible .text patches present in the 9c97 client. The executable input contract is now split into server/content/client/logh7-loopback-client-patch.json (bd192 -> 2848) and logh7-canonical-client-patch.json (2848 -> 9c97). Do not pass this receipt to exe-patch.mjs as an apply manifest.
provenanceCorrection: 2026-07-16 byte-for-byte reconstruction proved that 2848be76 is not pristine or an official update build. It is bd19263c with the 13-byte 202.8.80.179\0 slot replaced by 127.0.0.1 plus NUL padding.

targetExe.name: g7mtclient.exe
targetExe.note: Canonical 9c97 EXE equals the loopback-prepared 2848 build plus the 6 patches below (39 bytes, all in .text). The earlier claim that 127.0.0.1 was pristine/compiled-in is superseded: CD/install source bd192 contains 202.8.80.179\0 at raw offset 3599932, and the first reversible lineage stage replaces that 13-byte slot with 127.0.0.1 plus NUL padding.

sourceExe.name: g7mtclient.exe.bak-pre-font
sourceExe.note: Loopback-prepared project build, not pristine and not the official 2004 updater. originalBytes/rollbackBytes for the six .text patches below are measured from this stage; its own input is bd19263c as recorded by logh7-loopback-client-patch.json.

patches.reason: CreateFontA charset push 1 (DEFAULT_CHARSET) -> 0x80 (SHIFTJIS_CHARSET) so the client renders SJIS text. Applied byte: 0xaedec 01->80.
Second CreateFontA charset push 1 -> 0x80 (SHIFTJIS_CHARSET). Applied byte: 0xb0b98 01->80.

NOP the lobby FSM (FUN_0051a370) scene-active early-exit `je 0x51ba7a` so the FSM keeps ticking past state6 and conn2's recv pump stays alive to read the lobby RPC replies (0x2001/0x2004/0x2006/0x200a).
imm8 of `mov byte ptr [esp+0x15], 0` -> 1 inside the lobby/session-picker init (adjacent to session-change-opens-picker). Tracked as the 'item1' patch (backup g7mtclient.exe.pre-item1.bak).
FUN_0051a370 case 0x1c no-op -> `jmp 0x0051ad73` (case 0x19 picker init) + NOP tail, so a session change opens the session picker. Live-verified 2026-07-01.
NOP the transport router (0x6130a0) teardown `call 0x614b30` at 0x613150 so conn2 is NOT torn down on a null/unhandled frame; its recv pump keeps polling. conn1 closes via 0x4ac726, so conn1-safe.
